headline: CoreWeave, Nebius tumble as Meta threatens cloud
detail: Meta positioning as a cloud competitor hits AI infrastructure names broadly. Your book is heavy semis and chip ETFs - companies supplying AI compute (like SMH holdings) could see sentiment pressure if hyperscaler spending narratives shift. Not an immediate fundamental break, but a headwind for the sector mood today.
rec: No new buy triggered here - this is a sentiment watch on SMH and SOXL. If SMH starts breaking toward $540, that stop deserves a hard look.

headline: Jim Cramer: Use Wednesday rotation to your advantage
detail: Cramer flagging active rotation within markets, not a broad selloff. For a 90% tech/chip book on margin, rotation means some of your names get sold to fund others - creates chop and potential stop-hunts. Rotation days are exactly when leveraged ETF decay accelerates.
rec: No action on new buys. Use rotation chop as a reason to tighten awareness on SOXL ($200) and SPAL ($21.38) stops today, not to add.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce look like buys, analyst says
detail: Analyst calling NOW and CRM oversold, 'Armageddon fears too extreme.' NOW has an 8/12 scanner setup with entry 105.59-105.69, stop 104.82. Headline provides a real fundamental reason matching the mechanical pattern.
rec: NOW is worth watching for a long entry at scanner levels $105.59-$105.69, stop $104.82 - analyst catalyst plus scanner setup align. Small size only; daily trend is still down, so this is a counter-trend idea to consider, not a high-conviction add.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom
detail: Robotics theme plays directly into Nvidia and the broader chip supply chain - TSM, ARM, SMH components all benefit if this narrative gains traction. TSM has a 7/12 scanner setup. Longer-term tailwind for your existing holdings.
rec: No immediate buy - TSM scanner entry $443.34-$443.79 stop $442.47 is tight and the 1H is down. File it as a watchlist level; let price come to the setup before touching it.
